diagnostics.title: Server fetch follows redirects for caller-shaped URL
diagnostics.message: Server-side fetch code appears to follow redirects for a URL shaped like caller-controlled input.
diagnostics.help: Use `redirect: "manual"` or equivalent and re-validate every redirect target before following it to avoid SSRF redirect bypasses.

diagnostics.title: Filesystem path uses caller input
diagnostics.message: Filesystem access appears to use request, query, params, or body data as part of the path.
diagnostics.help: Resolve paths against a fixed base directory, reject traversal after normalization, and map user-visible identifiers to server-owned paths.

diagnostics.title: Weak cryptography in security context
diagnostics.message: Code uses weak hashes, deprecated ciphers, timing-unsafe comparisons, or Math.random in a security-shaped context.
diagnostics.help: Use modern primitives, `crypto.randomBytes` / Web Crypto randomness, and timing-safe comparisons for signatures, digests, tokens, and auth material.

diagnostics.title: HTML injection sink with dynamic content
diagnostics.message: HTML is injected from a dynamic-looking source, which can become XSS if the value is user-controlled or unsanitized.
diagnostics.help: Prefer rendering structured React nodes. If HTML is required, sanitize with a well-reviewed sanitizer and keep the trust boundary close to the sink.
